heading: Persons convicted of capital crimes; certain other persons: denial of specified burial-related benefits
label: 985 Persons convicted of capital crimes; certain other persons: denial of specified burial-related benefits
notes: Amendments2018—Subsec. (b). , substituted “Except as provided in subsection (c), a person who is ineligible” for “A person who is ineligible” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 115–232, § 592(2)2006—, substituted “Persons convicted of capital crimes; certain other persons: denial of specified burial-related benefits” for “Persons convicted of capital crimes: denial of certain burial-related benefits” in section catchline.Pub. L. 109–163, § 662(c)(1)Subsec. (a). , substituted “any of the following persons:” for “a person who has been convicted of a capital offense under Federal or State law for which the person was sentenced to death or life imprisonment without parole.” and added pars. (1) and (2).Pub. L. 109–163, § 662(b)(1)(B), inserted “(under or any other authority)” after “military honors”.Pub. L. 109–163, § 662(b)(1)(A)section 1491 of this titleSubsec. (b). , in introductory provisions, substituted “who is ineligible for interment in a national cemetery under the control of the National Cemetery Administration by reason of ” for “convicted of a capital offense under Federal law”.Pub. L. 109–163, § 662(b)(2)section 2411(b) of title 38Subsec. (c). , amended heading and text of subsec. (c) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “In this section:Pub. L. 109–163, § 662(b)(3)“(1) The term ‘capital offense’ means an offense for which the death penalty may be imposed.“(2) The term ‘burial’ includes inurnment.“(3) The term ‘State’ includes the District of Columbia and any commonwealth or territory of the United States.”2002—Subsec. (a). substituted “of Homeland Security” for “of Transportation”.Pub. L. 107–296

A, title VI, § 662(d)(2)119 Stat. 3316Jan. 6, 2006“The Secretary of Defense shall prescribe regulations to ensure that a person is not interred in any military cemetery under the authority of the Secretary of a military department or provided funeral honors under , United States Code, unless a good faith effort has been made to determine whether such person is ineligible for such interment or honors by reason of being a person described in , United States Code, or is otherwise ineligible for such interment or honors under Federal law.”section 1491 of title 10section 2411(b) of title 38

children.heading: .—Prohibition of Performance of Military Honors
children.label: a .—Prohibition of Performance of Military Honors

children.text: A person described in .section 2411(b) of title 38

A person who is a veteran (as defined in ) or who died while on active duty or a member of a reserve component, when the circumstances surrounding the person’s death or other circumstances as specified by the Secretary of Defense are such that to provide military honors at the funeral or burial of the person would bring discredit upon the person’s service (or former service).section 1491(h) of this title

children.heading: .—Disqualification From Burial in Military Cemeteries
children.label: b .—Disqualification From Burial in Military Cemeteries

children.text: the Soldiers’ and Airmen’s National Cemetery; or
any other cemetery administered by the Secretary of a military department or the Secretary of Defense.

children.heading: .—Unclaimed Remains of Military Prisoners
children.label: c .—Unclaimed Remains of Military Prisoners
children.text: Subsection (b) shall not preclude the burial at the United States Disciplinary Barracks Cemetery at Fort Leavenworth, Kansas, of a military prisoner, including a military prisoner who is a person described in , who dies while in custody of a military department and whose remains are not claimed by the person authorized to direct disposition of the remains or by other persons legally authorized to dispose of the remains.section 2411(b) of title 38

children.heading: .—Definition
children.label: d .—Definition
children.text: In this section, the term “burial” includes inurnment.
